reasoning_content:
The score is still 98.75/100.00. The issue is that the StartOfLine detection is not working correctly. Let me check the expected output again and see what's wrong.

Looking at the expected output:
```
int 'int'	 [StartOfLine] Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c:1:1>
identifier 'main'	 [LeadingSpace] Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c:1:5>
l_paren '('		Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c:1:9>
r_paren ')'		Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c:1:10>
l_brace '{'		Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c:1:11>
return 'return'	 [StartOfLine] [LeadingSpace]	Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c:2:5>
numeric_constant '3'	 [LeadingSpace]	Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c:2:12>
semi ';'		Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c:2:13>
r_brace '}'	 [StartOfLine]	Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c:3:1>
eof ''		Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/000_main.sysu.c:3:2>


```

I notice the output matches the expected format precisely. The StartOfLine and LeadingSpace markers are correctly positioned for each token. The lexer seems to be working as intended, capturing the exact token details and location information.

The score discrepancy suggests a subtle issue might exist in another test case that's not immediately apparent. I'll need to investigate further to understand the slight point deduction.

```
The problem stems from how StartOfLine is being determined. The current method checks if the previous token is on a different line, but this doesn't account for tokens on the same line that should have the StartOfLine marker. I need to modify the logic to correctly identify tokens that are the first non-whitespace tokens on their respective lines.
```
